data.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. On a macOS host with no util-linux flock binary (no Homebrew util-linux), or anywhere with REBAR_FORCE_MKDIR_LOCK=1 exported for the bash side only.
2. Run a bash-path write (e.g. `rebar comment <id> x`, which goes through ticket-lib.sh _flock_stage_commit) concurrently with a transition/claim (which executes src/rebar/_engine/ticket_txn.py).
3. Observe the two critical sections do not mutually exclude: _flock_stage_commit takes the mkdir lock (.ticket-write.lock.d, ticket-lib.sh:166-194) while ticket_txn.py takes fcntl.flock LOCK_EX on .ticket-write.lock (ticket_txn.py:54-60) — and mkdir-based locking does not contend with fcntl.flock (acknowledged at ticket-lib.sh:233-234, 310-311 for the _flock_write_json case, but _flock_stage_commit still uses mkdir).

Expected: all writers on one clone serialize on one lock (invariant I5: single locked write path).
Actual: on the mkdir-fallback platform class, a leaf write and a transition/claim can interleave inside the git add/commit critical section. Event files themselves are safe (unique names, I2), but concurrent `git commit` in one worktree can fail on .git/index.lock and trip the 'git commit failed while holding lock' error path; the optimistic re-read in ticket_txn.py is also not actually serialized against bash writers there.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One lock discipline serializes ALL same-clone writers on every platform class (flock(1), fcntl-only, no-flock)
- [ ] Regression test: mixed bash-write + ticket_txn.py claim storm under REBAR_FORCE_MKDIR_LOCK shows strict mutual exclusion (no index.lock failures, no lost commits)
- [ ] Fix lands no later than Tier D of adult-oxide-slave (docs/bash-migration.md section 6 carries the dual-window interop rule)
data.title: Lock-mechanism mismatch: bash mkdir-fallback writes do not contend with ticket_txn.py fcntl.flock on flock(1)-less platforms
